rationale: Operationalizes the required risk assessment methodology (likelihood vs. impact) for all systems including AI to identify threats to ePHI.
Governs the lifecycle of policy acknowledgement and training completion tracking required to prove workforce Awareness.
Establishes the resilience and recovery protocols (Data Backup Disaster Recovery) necessary to restore ePHI availability.
Enforces the Vendor Procurement Plan and specifically tracks the execution of Business Associate Agreements (BAAs).
Operationalizes Authorization/Supervision and Termination Procedures for workforce members accessing ePHI.
Enforces Isolating Health Care Clearinghouse Functions and Access Authorization logic.
Mandates the periodic technical and non-technical evaluation of the security posture against environmental changes.
Covers the authorization logic for accessing secure physical environments where ePHI is housed.
Establishes the policy for acceptable use of workstations to ensure screens and data are not exposed.
Enforces technical safeguards (like privacy screens or locking mechanisms) on physical computing assets.
Operationalizes Disposal and Media Re-use requirements ensuring ePHI is purged before hardware retirement.
Mandates unique user identification and emergency access procedures to prevent shared accounts.
Requires the technical implementation of session timeouts to prevent unauthorized access on unattended terminals.
Establishes the log retention and examination protocols necessary for forensic audit trails of ePHI activity.
Manages cryptographic hashing to validate that health data has not been improperly altered.
Requires technical methods to corroborate that ePHI has not been altered or destroyed in an unauthorized manner.
Mandates the use of unique authentication (passwords biometrics) to verify identity.
Mandates encryption standards (TLS 1.2+) for ePHI data in transit satisfying the Addressable specification.
Governs the documentation and mandatory 6-year retention of all administrative policies.
Establishes the formal regulatory reporting protocol to HHS/OCR and patients within the 60-day window.
Operationalizes data minimization by enforcing strict retention schedules and access limits.
Ensures ePHI is classified and indexed enabling the organization to fulfill patient access requests.
Governs the lifecycle and accuracy of the external Notice of Privacy Practices (NPP).
Formally designates the Privacy Official and Security Official required by law.
